title: harmala daurica
intro: Harmala daurica, also known as Syrian rue, is a perennial plant native to the Middle East and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include the presence of harmine and tetrahydroharmine, alkaloids that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Harmala daurica include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, as well as its use in treating conditions such as insomnia and epilepsy, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried herb or in capsule form, and it can also be infused as a tea or used as a tincture.
